title: Systems and methods for loading features
abstract: Systems, methods, and non-transitory computer-readable media can determine a set of feature components corresponding to a software application, wherein each feature component corresponds to a modularized feature provided by the software application. An order for loading the set of feature components upon execution of the software application can be determined. The set of feature components can be loaded in memory based at least in part on the determined order.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A computer-implemented method comprising: determining, by a computing device, a set of feature components corresponding to a software application, wherein each feature component corresponds to a modularized feature provided by the software application; determining, by the computing device, an order for loading the set of feature components upon execution of the software application; and causing, by the computing device, the set of feature components to be loaded in memory based at least in part on the determined order. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: assigning, by the computing device, each feature component to a tier in a set of tiers, wherein each tier is associated with a respective load order. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, wherein each feature component is assigned to a tier based at least in part on its load priority. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 3, wherein the load priority is determined based at least in part on historical user interaction data for a feature corresponding to the feature component. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: determining, by the computing device, a set of attributes describing a user operating the computing device; and assigning, by the computing device, a respective load order to each feature component based at least in part on the set of attributes. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: determining, by the computing device, a geographic region associated with a user operating the computing device; and 

 assigning, by the computing device, a respective load order to each feature component based at least in part on the geographic region. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: determining, by the computing device, a personalized load order for a user operating the computing device, the personalized load order being determined based at least in part on the user's historical interaction with features provided by the software application. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein each feature component is associated with a corresponding custom class loader. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the custom class loader is implemented using DexClassLoader. 1 0. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein each feature component corresponds to a respective Davlik Executable (dex) file. 1 1 . A system comprising: at least one processor; and a memory storing instructions that, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the system to perform: determining a set of feature components corresponding to a software application, wherein each feature component corresponds to a modularized feature provided by the software application; determining an order for loading the set of feature components upon execution of the software application; and causing the set of feature components to be loaded in memory based at least in part on the determined order. 1 2. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further causes the system to perform: 

 assigning each feature component to a tier in a set of tiers, wherein each tier is associated with a respective load order. 1 3. The system of claim 12, wherein each feature component is assigned to a tier based at least in part on its load priority. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the load priority is determined based at least in part on historical user interaction data for a feature corresponding to the feature component. 1 5. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further causes the system to perform: determining a set of attributes describing a user operating the computing device; and assigning a respective load order to each feature component based at least in part on the set of attributes. 1 6. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium including instructions that, when executed by at least one processor of a computing system, cause the computing system to perform a method comprising: determining a set of feature components corresponding to a software application, wherein each feature component corresponds to a modularized feature provided by the software application; determining an order for loading the set of feature components upon execution of the software application; and causing the set of feature components to be loaded in memory based at least in part on the determined order. 1 7. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 16, wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further causes the computing system to perform: assigning each feature component to a tier in a set of tiers, wherein each tier is associated with a respective load order. 1 8. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein each feature component is assigned to a tier based at least in part on its load priority. 1 9. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 18, wherein the load priority is determined based at least in part on historical user interaction data for a feature corresponding to the feature component. 20. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 16, wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further causes the computing system to perform: determining a set of attributes describing a user operating the computing device; and assigning a respective load order to each feature component based at least in part on the set of attributes. 21 . A computer-implemented method, in particular aimed at speeding up the loading and execution of a software application, comprising: determining, by a computing device, a set of feature components corresponding to a software application, wherein each feature component corresponds to a modularized feature provided by the software application and/or to a hardware component of the computing device; determining, by the computing device, an order for loading the set of feature components upon execution of the software application; and causing, by the computing device, the set of feature components to be loaded in memory based at least in part on the determined order. 22. The computer-implemented method of claim 21 , wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: determining, by the computing device, that a feature component or a set of feature components is or are not required and/or not supported by the computing device; and 

 causing, by the computing device, the determined feature component or set of feature components not to be loaded in memory. 23. The computer-implemented method of claim 21 or 22, wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: assigning, by the computing device, each feature component to a tier in a set of tiers, wherein each tier is associated with a respective load order; optionally, wherein each feature component is assigned to a tier based at least in part on its load priority; optionally, wherein multiple feature components can be assigned to the same tier (i.e. batched) based at least in part on their load priority and then can be loaded essentially in parallel. 24. The computer-implemented method of claim 23, wherein the load priority is determined based at least in part on historical user interaction data for a feature corresponding to the feature component. 25. The computer-implemented method of any of claims 21 to 24, wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: determining, by the computing device, a set of attributes describing a user operating the computing device and/or a set of attributes describing the computing device; and assigning, by the computing device, a respective load order to each feature component based at least in part on the set of attributes. 26. The computer-implemented method of claim 25, wherein a set of user attributes comprises one or more of the following: gender, age, preferred language, geographic region, information associated with a social networking system (e.g. social connections, social interactions, locations, geo-fenced areas, maps, places, events, pages, groups, posts, communications, content, feeds, account settings, privacy settings, a social graph, and various other types of data, work experience, educational history, hobbies or preferences, biographic information, demographic information, and various other types of 

 descriptive information, a user's privacy setting to regulate access to information about the user, a user's security setting to regulate access to software- and or hardware-components of a device; and/or wherein a set of computing device attributes comprise one or more of the following: device type, camera interface, messaging interface, content feed, operating system, network, communications technologies (e.g. available bandwidth), communication protocols, supported and/or installed applications, supported and/or installed application frameworks, processor, cache, input/output (I/O) bus (e.g. available bandwidth), host bridge, system memory, network interface, video memory, display device, mass storage, keyboard, pointing device, required and/or installed drivers. 27. The computer-implemented method of any of claims 21 to 26, wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: determining, by the computing device, a geographic region associated with a user operating the computing device; and assigning, by the computing device, a respective load order to each feature component based at least in part on the geographic region. 28. The computer-implemented method of any of claims 21 to 27, wherein determining the order for loading the set of feature components further comprises: determining, by the computing device, a personalized load order for a user operating the computing device, the personalized load order being determined based at least in part on the user's historical interaction with features provided by the software application. 29. The computer-implemented method of any of claims 21 to 28, wherein each feature component is associated with a corresponding custom class loader; optionally, wherein the custom class loader is implemented using DexClassLoader. 30. The computer-implemented method of any of claims 21 to 29, wherein each feature component corresponds to an individual executable file, e.g. a respective Davlik Executable (dex) file; optionally, wherein the files are loaded into memory one after the other in the determined load order. 31 . The computer-implemented method of any of claims 21 to 30, wherein each feature component corresponds to an individual executable file including software code corresponding to a feature along with the feature's exclusive dependencies; optionally, wherein shared dependencies (e.g., dependencies shared with the main component and/or a feature's exclusive dependencies) can still be included in a main executable file. 32. A system comprising: at least one processor; and a memory storing instructions that, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the system to perform a method according to any of claims 21 to 31 . 33. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium including instructions that, when executed by at least one processor of a computing system, cause the computing system to perform a method according to any of claims 21 to 31 . 34. A computer program product, preferably comprising a computer- readable non-transitory storage media, that is operable when executed on a data processing system to perform a method according to any of claims 21 to 31 .
